channel.title: +MT on Vishvas's notes
channel.description: Recent content in +MT on Vishvas's notes

item.title: Iran sympathy
item.description:
Source: TW

It is interesting to see that there is a massive expression of sympathy among the Turks for the Islamic Republic in Iran, though one is Sunni and the other Shi’a.

In the time of their Osman empire, the Turks were in a major rivalry with the Safavid empire in Iran.

Safavids & Turks

However, while “Persianate” the Safavid military power was primarily supported by the Turkic Turkoman tribes, led by the powerful Qizilbash (Red Headed Turks). While the state bureaucracy was still under the control of people of Persian ancestry, the mobile Turkic forces had almost a monopoly over military power. The Persian ancestry bureaucracy tried to offset this with the import of converted Caucasian slaves of Armenian and Georgian ancestry.
